note: Full article text is not retained here or anywhere in this repository (per ER-002-S1.1 Git tracking policy). Only outlet, paraphrased headline/gist, publish date, and URL are recorded.

sources.paraphrased_headline: Government announces overnight social-media limits and restrictions on addictive features for older teenagers
sources.paraphrased_gist: Apps will automatically apply a midnight-to-6 a.m. curfew to 16- and 17-year-olds, while certain engagement features will also be disabled by default.

sources.paraphrased_headline: Britain proposes a voluntary six-hour social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds
sources.paraphrased_gist: The measure is designed to protect sleep and concentration but can be switched off by users.

sources.paraphrased_headline: UK plans an overnight social-media limit for older teenagers
sources.paraphrased_gist: The government had not yet named every covered app; the proposal would be a default setting rather than a mandatory ban.

sources.paraphrased_headline: Britain plans a default overnight social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds
sources.paraphrased_gist: The policy is an extension of Britain's planned under-16 social-media restrictions; additional controls will target features designed to keep users scrolling.
